text: UTF-16 position conversion is subtly wrong for non-BMP characters.
mitigation: Add explicit tests for non-BMP strings (emoji and surrogate-pair identifiers). Document any known limitation in docs/ast-mcp-v1/position-encoding.md.
Pin exact versions in Cargo.toml. Run the full test suite on a clean cargo update -p <crate>. Record any pinned version in decisions.md.
Every truncated response carries truncated:true and returned:<n>. Add an integration test that asserts the flag is set when limits are hit.

text: Never-write-files is broken by a future refactor.
mitigation: Architectural lint test tests/architecture/no_write.rs scans the source tree for fs::write, tokio::fs::write, OpenOptions::write, and rename. Fails CI on any match.

text: Never-call-LSP is broken by a future refactor.
mitigation: Architectural lint test tests/architecture/no_lsp.rs scans Cargo.toml for lsp deps and src/ for use lsp_. Fails CI on any match (whitelist: tree-sitter).

text: Workspace path traversal via symbolic links.
mitigation: Resolve symlinks during path validation (std::fs::canonicalize) and re-check containment against the workspace root. Safety test creates a symlink to /etc/passwd and asserts rejection.

text: Query timeout is a soft limit, not a hard cancel.
mitigation: Run the query on a Tokio task and wrap the result with tokio::time::timeout. On timeout, return query_execution_failed with details.timeoutMs. Document the soft-vs-hard nature in the tool description.
Wrap parser invocation in catch_unwind and convert panics to parse_failed. Add a fuzz test corpus in phase 12.

text: Chunk IDs are not stable across versions.
mitigation: Document the ID format in docs/ast-mcp-v1/chunk-id.md and commit to backward compatibility for the id shape. If the format must change, version it (e.g., id = v2:...).
The V1 contract is explicit about scope and V2+ are deferred. V1 spec files reference the V1 tool surface only. specs.index.ndjson is namespaced to V1.

text: JSON payload size for full parse trees.
mitigation: includeTree is opt-in and bounded by maxNodes (500) and maxDepth (3). The default of includeTree:false is documented in tool descriptions and the spec.
